name: Apple Silicon QoS
overview: TrustDB achieves 9.1x lower CPU power consumption than SQLite on Apple Silicon by explicitly pinning I/O threads to efficiency cores via pthread_set_qos_class_self_np(QOS_CLASS_BACKGROUND). No competitor database has explicit Apple Silicon QoS integration — they all run on whatever cores the OS scheduler assigns, typically using high-performance cores for database I/O.

howItWorks.title: QoS Class Assignment
howItWorks.description: Database I/O threads are assigned QOS_CLASS_BACKGROUND via the macOS pthread API. This tells the OS scheduler to run these threads exclusively on efficiency (E) cores, not performance (P) cores.

howItWorks.title: E-Core Execution
howItWorks.description: Apple Silicon E-cores consume 3-10x less power than P-cores for equivalent work. Database I/O is typically not compute-bound — it's waiting for disk anyway — so E-cores provide adequate throughput at dramatically lower power.

howItWorks.title: Tiered Configuration
howItWorks.description: Two tiers are available: Tier 1 (maximum efficiency, 384mW) and Tier 2 (balanced, 437mW). Applications choose based on whether they prioritize minimum power or slightly better latency.

howItWorks.title: Transparent to Application
howItWorks.description: QoS pinning is handled internally by the storage engine. Applications using TrustDB get power efficiency automatically without any configuration.

comparisons.narrative: Every database on Apple Silicon currently runs at full P-core power because none explicitly manage QoS classes. TrustDB is the first embedded database to treat power efficiency as a first-class design goal on heterogeneous CPU architectures.

competitors.approach: No QoS awareness — runs on whatever cores the OS assigns
competitors.limitation: 3,995 mW CPU power (9.1x higher than TrustDB)
Thread pool runs on P-cores by default

competitors.approach: Apple framework, no explicit QoS for I/O threads
No QoS awareness for write transactions
